   * MAYBE : Updates for Blackwell : CUDA OptiX -  optix 9.1 cuda 13.1



:i:`Monitoring JUNOSW + Opticks : GPU ray trace accelerated optical photon simulation`
=========================================================================================

.. raw:: html

    <div class="mytitle">
    <header>
    <h1 style="background-color:lightgrey;text-align:center;">

        Monitoring <i>JUNOSW + Opticks</i> : GPU ray trace accelerated optical photon simulation
        <h2 style="background-color:lightgrey;text-align:center">
            Open source, https://github.com/simoncblyth/opticks
        </h2>
    </h1>
    </header>
    </div>
    <!--img style="position:absolute; top:400px; LEFT:100px; WIDTH:200px; " src="juno/JUNO_logo.png" >

     <!--p style="position:fixed;top:550px;left:100px;font-size:20px;background-color:white;border:1px solid black;padding:5px;width:200px;">
     On behalf of the JUNO Collaboration
     </p-->




    <div class="mycredit">
       <h2 style="background-color:lightgrey">
          Simon C Blyth, IHEP &mdash; JUNO Collaboration Meeting, IHEP, Beijing &mdash; (21 July 2026)
       </h2>
    </div>

.. s5_talk::

    Opticks is an open source project that applies GPU ray tracing to optical photon simulation
    and integrates that with Geant4. GPU parallelism gives drastic speedups of more than a factor of 1000.
    This render shows the photons resulting from a muon crossing the JUNO scintillator,
    each line represents a single photon.



.. comment

      * Opticks Optical simulation directly scales with ray tracing speed
      * Amdahl 'Law' : Overall Speedup S(p,n) for Parallel Fraction p and Parallized Speedup n
      * Updates for Blackwell : CUDA 13.1 OptiX 9.1


:i:`Outline`
-------------

.. image:: newtons-opticks.png
   :width: 299px
   :height: 547px
   :align: right


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:-5mm;" />

.. class:: small

    * [p2] OJ : Opticks+JUNOSW Automated Gitlab-CI/CD Releases
    * [p3] Optical Photon Simulation Context

      * (JUNO) Optical Photon Simulation Problem...
      * Four NVIDIA RTX Gen : Ray Tracing : ~2x every ~2 yrs
      * NVIDIA OptiX : Ray Tracing Engine -- Accessible GPU Ray Tracing
      * Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Hybrid Workflow

    * [p7] Opticks + JUNOSW (OJ) : Testing as Geometry and Models continue to Evolve

      * Opticks+JUNOSW Monitoring
      * gitlab-ci in OJ repository
      * Opticks Grafana Dashboard
      * Goal of Monitoring

    * [p12] Preparations for Three Species LS model

      * MC Method : Sampling using Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF)
      * Generation of scintillator wavelengths on device using GPU texture
      * Scint wavelength generation in triplicate (LAB, bisMSB, PPO)
      * One billion wavelengths Chi2 check
      * Status of Three Species LS model Preparations

    * [p20] Opticks Updates for CUDA 13.1 OptiX 9.1

    * [p21] Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Production Scaling ?

      * Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Monolith x4, x16
      * OpticksClients + OpticksService : Share GPUs
      * Opticks "Optical Core" => Server, "Periphery" => Client
      * NP_CURL.h : Array transport via HTTP POST
      * Opticks Server Prototype : python + FastAPI + nanobind + CSGOptiX
      * NVIDIA Triton Inference Server (aka Dynamo-Triton)

    * [p30] Summary + Links

.. comment

    .. raw:: html

       <hr/>



.. s5_talk::

   My focus today is on:

   * handling high energy events
   * scaling Opticks for monte carlo production





OJ : Opticks+JUNOSW Automated Gitlab-CI/CD Releases
-----------------------------------------------------

.. comment

    In [3]: import numpy as np ; np.array([1640,868])*0.25
    Out[3]: array([410., 217.])

    In [4]: import numpy as np ; np.array([1640,868])*0.3
    Out[4]: array([492. , 260.4])

    In [5]: import numpy as np ; np.array([1640,868])*0.27
    Out[5]: array([442.8 , 234.36])

    In [6]: import numpy as np ; np.array([1640,868])*0.28
    Out[6]: array([459.2 , 243.04])



.. comment

   Möbius strip 

.. sidebar:: :small:`Continuous Integration+Delivery`

    .. image:: ci_cd/blackduck_cicd.png
       :width: 459px
       :height: 243px
       :align: center


.. class:: normal

   Everyday ~17:15 non-GPU gitlab-runners:

   * **OJ** : build + geometry conversion test
   * release -> ``/cvmfs/opticks.ihep.ac.cn/oj/``  

   :r:`Non-GPU runners limit testing`

   1st build after Opticks release eg ``Opticks-v0.6.6``

   * -> dated **OJ** "reference" release  



.. raw:: html

   <pre class="mypre18">
   > realpath /cvmfs/opticks.ihep.ac.cn/oj/releases/LatestRelease/el9_amd64_gcc11/LastRef/envset.sh
   /cvmfs/opticks.ihep.ac.cn/oj/releases/J26.1.1_Opticks-v0.6.6/el9_amd64_gcc11/2026_06_22/envset.sh
   </pre>

.. class:: normal

   To use "LastRef" OJ release (from machine with GPU):

.. raw:: html

   <pre class="mypre18">
   source /cvmfs/opticks.ihep.ac.cn/oj/releases/LatestRelease/el9_amd64_gcc11/LastRef/envset.sh
   tut_detsim.py ... 
   </pre>



.. comment

   ## Latest build - with day (replaced the week after)
   realpath  /cvmfs/opticks.ihep.ac.cn/oj/releases/LatestRelease/el9_amd64_gcc11/Latest/envset.sh
   /cvmfs/opticks.ihep.ac.cn/oj/releases/J26.1.1_Opticks-v0.6.6/el9_amd64_gcc11/Thu/envset.sh




.. s5_talk::


   Using Opticks just requires a small modification to scripts, plus of course you 
   currently need access to an NVIDIA GPU 

   A very useful recent change development is integration of Opticks+JUNOSW Build, Test and Release
   into the GitLab CI/CD : continuous-integration/continuous-deployment.









`(JUNO) Optical Photon Simulation Problem...`
---------------------------------------------------------

.. raw:: html

     <p style="position:fixed;top:100px;left:900px;font-size:40px;background-color:white;border:1px solid black;padding:5px;">
     Opticks solves this using GPU ray tracing via NVIDIA OptiX
     </p>

     <p style="margin-bottom:7cm;" />


.. sidebar:: :small:`Huge CPU Memory+Time Expense`

    .. class:: small

         **JUNO Muon Simulation Bottleneck**
           >99% CPU time, memory constraints

         **Ray-Geometry intersection Dominates**
           simulation is not alone in this problem...

         **Optical photons : naturally parallel, simple :**
           * produced by Cherenkov+Scintillation
           * yield only Photomultiplier hits



.. s5_talk::

   Simulating many millions or even billions of optical photons per event
   is a huge processing and memory bottleneck.
   Opticks solves this optical photon simulation problem with GPU ray tracing from NVIDIA OptiX

.. comment

   * Serial optical simulation is hugely expensive.
   * Opticks applies massively parallel GPU ray tracing, to solve the optical simulation problem
   * For many experiments Opticks can prevent optical photons from limiting simulation







.. comment

    :small:`NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell`
    -------------------------------------------


    .. class:: small

        Blackwell(4th Gen RTX) vs Ada(3rd Gen RTX):

        * :r:`fourth-gen RT Cores : ray tracing ~doubled`
        * 96GB GDDR7 : internal bandwidth ~doubled (1792 GB/s)
        * PCIe 5.0 : external bandwidth ~doubled
        * fifth-gen Tensor Cores : add FP4 (4-bit float), AI ~doubled
        * add Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) support

          - virtual "split" into four instances

        * **power doubled** (600W)



    .. raw:: html

         <p style="position:fixed;top:1px;left:700px;font-size:30px;background-color:white;border:1px solid black;padding:5px;">
         Server Edition
         </p>



    .. s5_talk::

        NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell is from the 4th RTX generation.
        Compared to the 3rd generation there has been a doubling
        of ray tracing performance and also doubling in other areas
        such as internal and external bandwidth.


Four NVIDIA RTX Gen : Ray Tracing : :b:`~2x every ~2 yrs`
----------------------------------------------------------------------


.. comment

    :widths: auto
    :widths: auto


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:1cm;" />


.. table::
    :class: center-aligned-table
    :widths: 5 10 3 3 3 5 3 4 4
    :align: center

    +--------------+-----------------+---------+------------+---------+----------------------------------------------+
    | Gen          |  Model          | Year    | VRAM       | CUDA    |   RT (Ray tracing)                           |
    |              |                 |         +------+-----+ Cores   +-------------+------------------+-------------+
    |              |                 |         | GB   | GB/s|         |    Cores    | TFLOPS           |   Rise      |
    +==============+=================+=========+======+=====+=========+=============+==================+=============+
    | Turing       | Quadro RTX 6000 |  2018   |  24  | 672 |  4,608  | 72          | ~34              | [1]         |
    +--------------+-----------------+---------+------+-----+---------+-------------+------------------+-------------+
    | Ampere       | RTX A6000       |  2020   |  48  | 768 | 10,752  | 84          | ~76              |  2.2x       |
    +--------------+-----------------+---------+------+-----+---------+-------------+------------------+-------------+
    | Ada          | RTX 6000 Ada    |  2023   |  48  | 960 | 18,176  | 142         | ~211             |  2.7x       |
    +--------------+-----------------+---------+------+-----+---------+-------------+------------------+-------------+
    | Blackwell    | RTX PRO 6000    |  2025   |  96  | 1792| 24,064  |  188        | ~380             |  1.8x       |
    +--------------+-----------------+---------+------+-----+---------+-------------+------------------+-------------+


.. class:: small

   * :b:`RT Cores: fixed-function ASICs dedicated to ray tracing : bbox intersection, ray-triangle intersection, BVH traversal, ...`


   .. admonition:: NVIDIA RT TFLOPS: synthetic ray trace metric -- :r:`Turing -> Blackwell : 11x`
       :class: hint

       (Equivalent FLOPs per Ray Intersection) x (Intersections per clock) x (Core Clock) x (Number of RT Cores)



.. class:: small

   [1] baseline : 2018 "World's First Ray-Tracing GPU -- 10 Gigarays/sec"





.. s5_talk::

    This table shows characteristics of four generations of RTX 6000 GPUs
    At each generation, every 2 years or so,  ray tracing performance has approximately doubled.
    Much of these gains are due to the RT cores, which are ray trace dedicated ASICs.






.. comment

   * Blackwell memory bandwidth x1.9 Ada (GDDR6->7)
   * Blackwell: supports PCIe 5.0 - doubling host-to-device transfer speed [with PCIe 5.0 motherboards] (Others: 3.0, 4.0, 4.0)

    https://www.techpowerup.com/gpu-specs/quadro-rtx-6000.c3307            24 GB, 6,299 USD  672.0 GB/s   4608
    https://www.techpowerup.com/gpu-specs/rtx-a6000.c3686                  48 GB, 4,649 USD  768.0 GB/s  10752
    https://www.techpowerup.com/gpu-specs/rtx-6000-ada-generation.c3933    48 GB, 6,799 USD  960.0 GB/s  18176
    https://www.techpowerup.com/gpu-specs/rtx-pro-6000-blackwell.c4272     96 GB, 8,565 USD  1792  GB/s  24064

    https://aecmag.com/workstations/review-nvidia-rtx-pro-blackwell-series-gpus/
    https://aecmag.com/workstations/review-nvidia-rtx-pro-blackwell-series-gpus

    nvidia-rtx-blackwell-gpu-architecture.pdf




.. comment


    :i:`AB_Substamp_ALL_Etime_vs_Photon_rtx_gen1_gen3.png`
    --------------------------------------------------------


    .. raw:: html

         <p style="margin-bottom:45mm;" />


    .. class:: tiny


        .. table:: Event Time(s) vs PH(M)
            :align: right

            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | PH(M)   |   G1   | G3  | G1/G3  |
            +=========+========+=====+========+
            |  1      |  0.47  |0.14 |  3.28  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 10      |  0.44  |0.13 |  3.48  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 20      |  4.39  |1.10 |  3.99  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 30      |  8.87  |2.26 |  3.93  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 40      | 13.29  |3.38 |  3.93  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 50      | 18.13  |4.49 |  4.03  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 60      | 22.64  |5.70 |  3.97  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 70      | 27.31  |6.78 |  4.03  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 80      | 32.24  |7.99 |  4.03  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 90      | 37.92  |9.33 |  4.06  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+
            | 100     | 41.93  |10.42|  4.03  |
            +---------+--------+-----+--------+




    .. raw:: html

        <p class="mymidleftbox">Optical simulation 4x faster 1st->3rd gen RTX </p>

        <p class="mybotrightbox">3rd gen Ada : 100M ph sim. in 10s [TMM PMT model, Custom CSG]</p>




    .. raw:: html

       <p class="floating-info-box">
          Opticks optical simulation speed directly scales with ray tracing speed.
       </p>

    .. raw:: html

        <p style="margin-bottom:17cm;" />


    .. class:: tiny
        :align: right

        TMM : Transfer-Matrix Method |nbsp10| |nbsp10|


    .. s5_talk::

        The plot shows kernel time vs number of photons for 1st and 3rd generation RTX GPUs.
        Optical simulation performance is a factor of four faster with the 3rd generation
        compared to the 1st.

        Opticks optical performance directly scales with ray tracing performance.







:small:`NVIDIA® OptiX™ Ray Tracing Engine -- Accessible GPU Ray Tracing`
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

.. sidebar:: :small:`Flexible Ray Tracing Pipeline`

    .. class:: small

        :g:`Green: User Programs`,  :e:`Grey: Fixed function/HW`

    .. image:: nvidia/optix7/OptiX-API.png
       :width: 400px
       :align: right

    .. class:: small

        :b:`Analogous to OpenGL rasterization pipeline`


.. class:: small

   **OptiX makes GPU ray tracing accessible**

   * :r:`Programmable GPU-accelerated Ray-Tracing Pipeline`
   * Single-ray shader programming model using CUDA
   * ray tracing acceleration using RT Cores (RTX GPUs)
   * "...free to use within any application..."

   **OptiX features**

   * stateless, multi-threaded and asynchronous
   * built-in triangles, user-defined custom primitives
   * acceleration structure creation + traversal (eg BVH)
   * instanced geometry + acceleration structures
   * compiler optimized for GPU ray tracing

.. class:: small

   **User provides (Green):**

   * ray generation, geometry bounding boxes
   * custom primitive intersect functions
   * instance transforms

   Latest Release : :r:`NVIDIA® OptiX™ 9.1.0 (Dec 2025)`

   * :b:`NVIDIA R590+ driver`
   * OptiX 6 support removed from drivers R590+



.. s5_talk::

   NVIDIA OptiX gives access to the accelerated ray tracing.
   The implementation is within the NVIDIA Driver


.. comment

   NVIDIA OptiX provides an API that makes GPU ray tracing accessible

   * it divides up the ray tracing workflow
   * the green boxes represent user provided CUDA programs, including:

     * ray generation : where Opticks generates photons and steers the simulation
     * intersection : where the CSG geometry is implemented

   * geometry has to be translated into a GPU appropriate easily serialized form






Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Hybrid Workflow
-------------------------------------------------------------

.. class:: small

    *Opticks* enables Geant4 based simulation to offload optical photon simulation to the GPU

.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:13cm;" />

.. class:: normal

    **NVIDIA GPU ray tracing of billions[1] of rays per second applied to optical simulation**

.. class:: tiny

    [1] Actual performance depends on geometry and its modelling, JUNO optical simulation speedups > 1000x Geant4 have been measured


.. s5_talk::

    Opticks integrates OptiX ray tracing with Geant4 simulation.

    * at initialization Opticks translates the Geant4 geometry and uploads it to the GPU

    * Geant4 processes that normally generate optical photons are modified
      to instead give gensteps which parameterize how to generate photons
      and those gensteps are uploaded to the GPU at the end of each event

    * optical simulation is entirely offloaded to the GPU,
      only non-culled hits require CPU memory allocation

..  comment

   * Opticks bridges between Geant4 on the CPU and NVIDIA OptiX GPU ray tracing
   * Thus crucially requires translation of the geometry into a GPU appropriate form.




.. comment


    :i:`amdahl300`
    --------------


    .. comment

        Amdahl 'Law' : Overall Speedup S(p,n) for Parallel Fraction p and Parallized Speedup n

        Using less resources for each photon means more can be in flight at the same time
        that gives more speedup.



    .. s5_talk::

        Large optical speedup factors in the thousands are due to GPU parallelism.
        This shows the overall speedup vs the parallelized speedup for different parallel fractions.

        Overall speedup is limited by the non-parallelized parts of the simulation.
        That is everything other than the optical photons.

        After the time for the photons is parallelized down to zero
        you are still left with the rest of the simulation,
        which determines the overall speedup.


    .. comment

        The speedup of the optical photon simulation can be more than
        1000x Geant4, but the non-optical part of the event is not parallelized.
        Performance depends on the fraction of the compute that can be
        parallelized. Overall speedups of 100x are expected.

        The red curve is for a parallel fraction of 0.99
        with a parallelized speedup of 1000x the overall
        speedup is around 90x

        The benefits of going much beyond 1000x are small
        for traditional uses of optical photon simulation.

    .. comment

        How much parallelized speedup actually useful to overall speedup?

        **Overall speed limited by serial portion**

        *P*
             parallelizable proportion
        *1-P*
             non-parallelizable portion
        *n*
             parallel speedup factor


        optical photon simulation, P ~ 99% of CPU time

        * => limit on overall speedup S(n) is 100x
        * even with parallel speedup factor >> 1000x
        * :b:`speedup beyond 1000x not needed`

    .. comment

        Some novel ideas for how to use ultra fast
        optical simulation need more performance.

        **Speculative+novel uses require ultra-extreme speedup**

        * GPU integration to generate PDFs
        * "simulation-as-reconstruction" (direct ML fits)







Opticks+JUNOSW (OJ) Monitoring
----------------------------------

.. class:: normal

    :b:`Opticks : fast replacement for Geant4 optical sim,` :r:`BUT FRAGILE:`

.. class:: normal

    * model/geometry change often needs CUDA development 

      * eg: new three species LS model with state transitions

    * :r:`any change can break correctness + performance`

      * => :b:`REQUIRES CONTINUOUS MONITORING`

    **Accelerate dev-cycle via automated : Validation + Performance Mon.**   

.. class:: normal

    **OJ + Opticks Monitoring** : https://code.ihep.ac.cn/blyth/oj/

    * gitlab-ci nightly tests - (*gitlab-ci coordination, Grafana presentation*)
    * GPU Workstation: gitlab-runner + **Grafana** server
    * *Opticks:* :b:`summarize` test performance/status into SQLite3 DB tables
    * visualize using **Grafana** Dashboard

      * interactive plotting via SQL queries => dynamic plots

    "Pipeline" works, NEXT: extend coverage to all important tests   




.. s5_talk::

   JUNO geometry and model continue to evolve

   * making validation and testing an ongoing requirement

   Recent progress in automated testing is to setup a gitlab CI runner
   on a GPU workstation to help with promptly finding issues with
   both the Geant4 geometry and with the translation of it.




:i:`opticks_monitoring_gitlab_ci_20260716_141838.png` 
------------------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:100mm;" />


.. sidebar:: OJ repository

    .. class:: small

       ``https://code.ihep.ac.cn/blyth/oj`` 
           Opticks + JUNOSW test scripts

       * daily testing by GPU Workstation gitlab-runner
       * :r:`report => summary into SQLite3 DB table`  
       * Grafana result visualization


.. s5_talk::

   * gitlab-ci for the OJ repository coordinates which scripts to run and presents pass/fail

----------------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:140mm;" />


``Opticks Grafana Dashboard``
    * ``1. interactive time window``
    * ``2. direct links from gitlab-ci``

   Grafana presents test results in a Dashboard

----------------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:110mm;" />


``Web Interface -> Edit Grafana viz of "cxs_min.sh" - GPU time vs photon``
   * ``Viz based on SQL queries from SQLite3 DB tables``
   * ``Grafana very popular => AI very helpful``


.. s5_talk::

   The dashboard is edited via a web interface. The data to plot comes from SQL queries against SQLite DB.



.. comment

    grafana
    --------

    ``Grafana Dashboard Examples : popular tool => AI helps greatly`` 



Opticks+JUNOSW (OJ) Monitoring : Goal
---------------------------------------

.. class:: normal

   :b:`Nightly : DETAILED Validation + Performance Monitoring`

   Requires, for all important tests:

   1. expand DB schema to hold **summary results**

      * tables : ``~/opticks/sysrap/sreportdb.sql`` 

   2. add reporting stage that writes **summary results** into DB tables 

      * python OR C++ using ``~/np/NSQLite.h``
      * insert into tables : ``~/opticks/sysrap/sreportdb.h``
      * control script : ``~/opticks/sysrap/tests/sreportdb.sh``

   3. add Grafana visualization of results + alerts for deviations

      * use web interface to develop SQL query + configure viz 


   * currently ``cxs_min.sh`` added to develop the "pipeline"
   * straightforward to incorporate more tests
   * trying json "metadata" field - to avoid too many schema changes
   * sqlite3 : ``json_extract(metadata, '$.photon_counts.total')``
   


.. s5_talk::

   GOAL



:i:`Preparations for Three Species LS model`  
--------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:40mm;" />


.. class:: huge

    Preparations for Three Species LS model

.. class:: normal

    * MC Method : Sampling using Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF)
    * Generation of scintillator wavelengths on device using GPU texture
    * Scint wavelength generation in triplicate (LAB, bisMSB, PPO)
    * One billion wavelengths Chi2 check
    * Status of Three Species LS model Preparations


.. s5_talk::

    KLOP

 


:small:`MC Method : Sampling using Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF)`
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

.. image:: /env/presentation/normal_pdf_cdf.png
   :width: 400px
   :align: right

.. class:: small

   :red:`Aim : create sample (a set of values) that follows an analytic PDF`

   **Cumulative distribution function (CDF) : Prob( X < x)**

   * definite integral PDF f -> CDF F

     * **F(b) - F(a) = P( a < X <= b ) = Integral a->b  f(x) dx**      

   * monotonically increasing function, CDF(-inf) = 0, CDF(inf) = 1    

   **Map uniform randoms onto probabilities**

   1. integrate PDF -> CDF (mapping PDF domain onto probability [0,1] )
   2. invert the CDF, so domain becomes [0,1]  
   3. :b:`inverted_CDF(uniform random) -> sample value` 

   **Intuitively : throw randoms uniformly "vertically"**

   * Sigmoid CDF : low probability close to zero or 1 corresponding to tails  
   * greater probability in high PDF areas      
   * extreme case of delta-function PDF : all probability at one value

     * sigmoid CDF becomes Heaviside step-function

   :red:`CDF "encodes" shape of PDF in convenient form for sampling` 


.. class:: tiny

   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverse_transform_sampling


.. s5_talk::

    Reminder of the principal of sampling CDFs
 
    Using a GPU texture to hold the inverted CDF provides a very fast
    way to generate samples on the GPU simply by using a uniform
    random to do a lookup into the GPU texture.  




:small:`Generation of scintillator wavelengths on device using GPU texture`
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

.. image:: /env/presentation/inverted_cdf_GScintillatorLib_py.png
   :width: 500px
   :align: right

.. class:: small

   **GPU texture**
       1D/2D/3D data structure with highly optimized lookup
       using dedicated GPU hardware : Texture Mapping Units (TMUs)

   **Layered GPU texture (aka texture array or "atlas")**
       Single data structure holding multiple 1D/2D arrays, where
       layers are accessed with integer index


   **Current Opticks Scintillation model** 

   * fraction of absorbed photons "reincarnated" in same thread   
   * encode inverted CDF **ICDF** into (cudaTextureObject_t)scint_tex
   * uniform random lookups into scint_tex => wavelengths from distrib
 
   .. raw:: html

       <pre class="mypretiny">
       float u_reemit_prob = curand_uniform(&rng);  // pseudo-code
       if (u_reemit_prob < reemit_prob ) {  
           float u_wavelength = curand_uniform(&rng);
           p.wavelength = tex2D<float>(scint_tex, u_wavelength, 0.5f );   <b><span class="alarm"> # (cudaTextureObject_t)scint_tex lookup  </span></b>
           s.flag = BULK_REEMIT ;
           return CONTINUE;
       } else {   
           s.flag = BULK_ABSORB ;
           return BREAK;
       }    
       </pre>

.. class:: large

   https://github.com/simoncblyth/opticks/blob/master/qudarap/qscint.h
   
.. s5_talk::

    GPU ICDF sampling - yields wavelength from a random throw and texture lookup





:small:`Scint wavelength generation in triplicate (LAB, bisMSB, PPO)` 
----------------------------------------------------------------------


.. class:: small

   +----------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+  
   | U4ScintThree.h       | Geant4 : **ScintIntegral::GetEnergy** -> ICDF                               |     
   +----------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+  
   | QTexLayered.h        | CUDA layered texture from array (layers, height, width, payload )           |  
   +----------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+  
   | qscint_three.h       | wavelength_hd20  tex atlas lookup with random u0 -> wl                      | 
   +----------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+  
   | QScintThree.h        | * Scint ICDF texture atlas 3*3 layers  (3 species, 3 zone LHS/MID/RHS)      |
   |                      | * initialize qscint_three.h on CPU, upload to GPU                           | 
   +----------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+  

.. raw:: html

    <pre style="mypre8">0        e-m   e   e+m  ["HD20": e=.05,m=e/10]   -e-m 1-e 1-e+m        1
    |         |    |    |                             |    |    |          |
    +---LHS-------------+                             +----------RHS-------+
    |         +----:----------------MID--------------------:----+          |
    |              :    |        1 - 2*(e-m)          |    :               |
    |              :    |                             |    :               |
    |        u < e :    |     otherwise MID zone      |    : u > 1 - e     |                        
    |  x=u/(e+m)   :    | x = (u-(e-m))/(1-2*(e-m))   | x=(u-(1-e-m))/(e+m)|</pre>


.. class:: small

   * [u: uniform random 0->1] At edge (u < 0.05, u > 0.95) :  CDF flat => ICDF steep :  :r:`need ultra high resolution`
   * **"HD20"** 4096 bin, 20x at edge (but only 3x larger) => :b:`enables GPU wavelength lookup(float) to match Geant4 calc(double)` 
   * :b:`margins(m) prevent Chi2 spikes from float im-precision at zone boundaries`  

   ``float qscint_three::wavelength_hd20(int species_idx, float u ) const``

.. class:: large

   https://github.com/simoncblyth/opticks/blob/master/qudarap/qscint_three.h
 

.. s5_talk::

    Extend to three species using a nine layered texture, three for each species.
    Three probability zones are used to provide more bins at the edges, where
    the ICDF is very steep.



:i:`QScintThree_test_sh_COMP_012_LOGY1_EDGE1_DPI300`
----------------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:130mm;" />


.. class:: small

    ``1e9 wavelengths - (logscale) G4:Opticks diff not visible - Chi2/ndf < 1.001`` 


.. s5_talk::

    This compares histograms of a billion wavelengths generated with standard Geant techniques
    vs inverse CDF tex lookups on GPU.  No differences are visible, and Chi2/ndf is consistent with a match. 



:i:`QScintThree_test_sh_COMP_012_LOGY0_EDGE1_DPI300`
----------------------------------------------------



.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:130mm;" />


.. class:: small

    ``1e9 wavelengths - (linear) G4:Opticks diff not visible - Chi2/ndf < 1.001`` 


.. s5_talk::

    Linear version


:i:`QScintThree_test_sh_COMP_012_LOGY1_EDGE1_DPI300_BOFFSET1e7`
----------------------------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:130mm;" />


.. class:: small

    ``1e9 wavelengths - (lin+offset) G4:Opticks diff not visible - Chi2/ndf < 1.001`` 


.. s5_talk::

    As the distributions are on top of each other - adding an offset allows to see



Status of Three Species LS model Preparations
----------------------------------------------


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:10mm;" />


.. comment

    :class: center-aligned-table
    :align: center


.. table::
    :class: left-aligned-table
    :widths: 5, 1
    :align: center

    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+
    |  Aspect                                                         |  Status   |
    +=================================================================+===========+
    |  Wavelength generation in triplicate using 9 layer texture      |   |Y|     |
    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+
    |  Maintain species state for each photon                         |   |N|     |
    |                                                                 |           |
    |  * 2 bits in ``sphoton.h`` (set/get)_species                    |           |
    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+
    |  Species "Process" choice by distance comparison                |   |N|     | 
    |                                                                 |           |
    |  * needs to be optional without code duplication                |           |
    |  * (Opticks operates at photon level)                           |           |  
    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+
    |  Species transition probabilities + delays                      |   |N|     |       
    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+
    |  Attain match with JUNOSW/Geant4                                |   |N|     |
    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+
    |  Add test + Grafana dashboard panel to maintain match           |   |N|     | 
    +-----------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+



.. class:: normal

   Dingyong Yang has started looking into the missing aspects
 

.. s5_talk::

    Not yet merged



Opticks Updates for CUDA 13.1 OptiX 9.1
---------------------------------------------

.. class:: normal

    **Major CUDA upgrade** : supported from ``Opticks-v0.6.6`` 2026/06/18

    * NVIDIA CUDA 13.1 (December 4, 2025) -- "biggest expansion of CUDA since launch in 2006"

      * CMake machinery and thrust header changes required Opticks fixes
      * Support for eg: NVIDIA Blackwell RTX 6000

    * NVIDIA OptiX 9.1 (December 12, 2025)

      * OptiX : stricter kernel checks eg: UNSUPPORTED_PRIMITIVE_TYPE errors

    New Docker Image : ``el9.4-cuda13.1-opticks``

    * Accessible : https://hub.docker.com/r/simoncblyth/junosw/tags
    * Image used by JUNOSW gitlab-ci ``~/junosw/.gitlab-ci.yml``
    * ``~/junoenv/docker/Dockerfile-from-junosw-cudaxx-runtimeplus-el9``
    * Opticks Dockerfile now just adds CUDA to ``junosw/base:el9-9.4`` image created by Tao Lin 

      * => :b:`future CUDA updates should be simpler`

    * WIP: Performance testing of Opticks on NVIDIA Blackwell RTX 6000 

      * try to enable apples-to-apples comparisons despite continual geometry + model changes 


.. s5_talk::

    future CUDA updates should be simpler



.. comment

    Ada->Blackwell : current geom Blackwell vs old geom Ada : :r:`not doubled`
  
    * WIP: apples-to-apples comparison
    * eg: re-create old geometry from past GDML files



:i:`Scaling Opticks`
-----------------------


.. raw:: html

     <p style="margin-bottom:52mm;" />

.. class:: huge

    Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Production Scaling ?


.. class:: normal

   * hard to make good use of GPU resources, gets harder with each generation

     * :r:`under-utilized GPUs`

   * monolithic scaling Opticks ?

     * GPU idle while : Geant4 init + non-optical simulation : :r:`wasteful of GPU resources`

   :b:`=> Client-Server architecture`

   * separate GPU optical simulation into OpticksServer
   * share server between CPU nodes



.. s5_talk::

   How to scale Opticks is an open question

   Scaling monolithically means that GPUs sit idle while Geant4 is initialized and
   the non-optical simulation is done.

   Can server-client fix : lack of GPU resources + underutilized GPUs






:i:`Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Hybrid Workflow 2x2 ?`
--------------------------------------------------------------

.. raw:: html

     <p style="margin-bottom:82mm;" />

.. class:: huge

    Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Monolith x4 ?


.. s5_talk::

    Small MC productions could be done by scaling trivially.


:i:`Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Hybrid Workflow 4x4 ?`
---------------------------------------------------------------

.. raw:: html

     <p style="margin-bottom:82mm;" />

.. class:: huge

    Geant4 + Opticks + NVIDIA OptiX : Monolith x16 ?

.. raw:: html

     <p style="margin-bottom:36mm;" />

.. class:: right

    "Monolithic" scaling : :r:`very inefficient use of scarce GPU resources`


.. s5_talk::

    For large MC productions, scaling this way is not practical because
    its an extremely inefficient use the GPUs.






OpticksClients + OpticksService : Share GPUs
------------------------------------------------------



.. s5_talk::

    GPU-less clients sharing a service via HTTP POST allows increasing GPU utilization.


:i:`Client.png`
----------------


.. s5_talk::

    The aim is for large numbers of clients to share the service.
    Actually the more idle the GPUs are in the monolithic approach the
    greater the share factor can be.

    I think that even before developing an asynchronous compute service
    it should be possible to reach useful share factors.

    And those can be improved by putting in the effort of getting async compute to work,
    and also using multiple GPUs in the server.









:small:`Opticks "Optical Core" => Server, "Periphery" => Client`
----------------------------------------------------------------------


.. table::
    :class: center-aligned-table
    :widths: 2, 5, 1, 1
    :align: center

    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+
    | Package    |   Role                                           |   Client  |    Server |
    +============+==================================================+===========+===========+
    |  SysRap    |  Geometry and event types, array NP.hh           |   |Y|     |     |Y|   |
    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+
    |  CSG       |  CPU/GPU geometry model                          |   |Y|     |     |Y|   |
    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+
    | QUDARap    |  CUDA optical simulation                         |   |N|     |     |Y|   |
    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+
    | CSGOptiX   |  OptiX 7+ geometry, GPU ray trace                |   |N|     |     |Y|   |
    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+
    |  U4        |  Geometry convert, collect gensteps, return hits |   |Y|     |     |N|   |
    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+
    |  G4CX      |  Top level interface, acts via :r:`SSimulator`   |   |Y|     |     |N|   |
    +------------+--------------------------------------------------+-----------+-----------+


.. table::
    :class: center-aligned-table
    :widths: 2, 5, 4
    :align: center

    +---------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------+
    |                     | Client                                                      |   Server                        |
    +=====================+=============================================================+=================================+
    | :strike:`depends:`  | NVIDIA GPU + CUDA + OptiX                                   |  Geant4, U4, G4CX               |
    +---------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------+
    |  depends:           | libcurl 7.76.1+, NP_CURL.h                                  |  python, FastAPI, nanobind      |
    +---------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------+
    | :r:`SSimulator`:    | SOpticksClientSimulator                                     |  CSGOptiX                       |
    +---------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------+---------------------------------+


.. class:: normal

    Client build from common Opticks codebase with ``OPTICKS_CONFIG=Client``

    * :b:`OpticksClient use just like OpticksFull(monolithic) - simulate via network to GPU node`



.. s5_talk::

    Opticks is a collection of packages split by dependency, which makes it straightforward to divide into server and client.
    The client can be used just like the full opticks but no GPU is needed. It performs the simulation remotely.





NP_CURL.h : Array transport via HTTP POST
-----------------------------------------------

.. class:: normal

    :b:`Basis for Opticks Client` - using libcurl 7.76.1+ (2021) - default in many Linux distro


.. container:: center

    ``NP* NP_CURL::transformRemote( NP* a, size_t index, size_t count )``
      *HTTP POST array to endpoint, receive array in response, metadata in headers*



.. class:: normal
     :align: left

     ``NP.hh`` : C++ array with NumPy serialization (Opticks numerical base), ``NP_CURL.h`` headers:


.. table::
    :align: center
    :widths: auto
    :class: small center-aligned-table

    +-------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
    |  HTTP metadata headers        |   Note                                                            |
    +===============================+===================================================================+
    | x-opticks-shape               |  array shape eg "(10,6,4)" for 10 gensteps                        |
    +-------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
    | x-opticks-dtype               |  eg "float32"                                                     |
    +-------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
    | x-opticks-index               |  eg eventID controlling random number stream offsets              |
    +-------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
    | x-opticks-count               |  eg: number of photons in genstep, cost of request                |
    +-------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
    | x-opticks-meta                |  general eg geometry root node digest - :b:`assert same geometry` |
    +-------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+


.. class:: normal
     :align: left

     HTTP 429, 503 : Too Many Requests, Service Unavailable (temporary downtime)

     * follows ``Retry-After: <seconds>`` header suggestion


.. class:: tiny

     https://github.com/simoncblyth/np/blob/master/NP_CURL.h --  https://github.com/simoncblyth/np/blob/master/NP.hh


.. s5_talk::

     The Basis for the Client is this header which uses libcurl to upload gensteps in HTTP POST requests and accept hits in response.




:small:`Opticks Server Prototype : python + FastAPI + nanobind + CSGOptiX`
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

.. class:: normal

    ``CSGOptiX/tests/CSGOptiXService_FastAPI_test/CSGOptiXService_FastAPI_test.sh``

    * *FastAPI* : **ASGI** python web framework
    * *nanobind* : python <=> C++ (Using *uv*, :strike:`pip`)
    * *opticks/CSG* : load persisted geometry
    * *opticks/CSGOptiX* : simulate

    :r:`Prototype client + service operational`

    * :b:`load testing with k6`  https://k6.io
    * :r:`started adding concurrent CUDA support to Opticks`

      * WIP: extend async CUDA to all Opticks GPU usage

    **"Roll your own" Prototype Server : Educational, BUT:**

    * difficult to robustly serve many clients

      * => unsuitable for MC production campaign (?)

    * :b:`PLAN : compare with widely used open source server` : **NVIDIA Triton Inference Server**


.. raw:: html

    <p style="margin-bottom:10mm;" />



.. s5_talk::

    The server/client prototype operates. But perhaps rolling your own server
    might not be the best way to robustly serve many clients.




NVIDIA Triton Inference Server (aka Dynamo-Triton)
---------------------------------------------------

.. sidebar:: :small:`Potential basis for Opticks Server`

   .. class:: small

       * efficiently bring Opticks to non-GPU nodes
       * no language barrier + binding, no python GIL
       * mature Prometheus metrics monitoring
       * :b:`make good use of GPU resources`
       * serve more clients
       * **backends can work with non-NVIDIA GPUs**

.. class:: small

    Triton[1] : :r:`open-source`, designed to accelerate AI deployment at scale

    * despite "Inference", "AI" : is flexible async tensor server
    * :b:`NVIDIA maintained open source server software`
    * dynamic batching : requests queued and combined in batch
    * multi-GPU support, concurrency
    * widely used : tech, financial, manufacturing companies

    :r:`Wrap Opticks as Custom C++ Triton Backend "model(s)" ?`

    Make request load like inference : smaller, more uniform

    Make requests during genstep collection :
      * => tunable ``max_slots``
      * :b:`decouple compute from physics`

    High GPU utilization => concurrency :
      * async CUDA Opticks
      * async CUDA memory pools [2]

    Robust Server (MC campaign):
      * static flat VRAM
      * backpressure, signal client retry



.. class:: tiny

    [1] https://developer.nvidia.com/dynamo-triton

    [2] ``cudaMallocFromPoolAsync``


.. comment

    https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/ai/dynamo-triton/


.. s5_talk::

   Triton is an open source inference server maintained by NVIDIA



.. comment

    Other Progress
    ----------------

    .. class:: small

       * gitlab-ci runner setup on GPU workstation in progress

         * automated GPU Opticks unit tests
         * automated GPU Opticks+JUNOSW (OJ) integration tests

           * dedicated OJ repository for test scripts


       * Enhanced 2D simtrace




:small:`Summary and Links`
----------------------------------------

.. sidebar:: :small:`Extra Benefits of Adopting Opticks`

   .. class:: small

      * :b:`high performance novel visualization`
      * detailed photon instrumentation, validation
      * comparisons find issues with both simulations:

        * complex geometry, overlaps, bugs...

      :r:`=> using Opticks improves CPU simulation too !!`

.. raw:: html

     <p style="margin-bottom:5mm;" />

..

  *Opticks* : state-of-the-art GPU ray traced optical simulation integrated with *Geant4*

..

  GPU-less *OpticksClient* + *OpticksService* in development,
  bringing *Opticks* everywhere

..

  *OpticksMonitoring* with gitlab-ci + Grafana to
  speedup dev cycle via nightly GPU tests  


.. raw:: html

     <p style="margin-bottom:3mm;" />

.. class:: normal

  * NVIDIA Ray Trace Performance continues rapid progress (2x each gen., every ~2 yrs)
  * **any simulation limited by optical photons can benefit from Opticks**

.. comment

  * more photon limited -> more overall speedup (99% -> ~90x)

.. table::
    :align: center

    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+
    | https://github.com/simoncblyth/opticks           | day-to-day code repository              |
    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+
    | https://simoncblyth.github.io                    | presentations and videos                |
    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+
    | https://groups.io/g/opticks                      | forum/mailing list archive              |
    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+
    | email: ``opticks+subscribe@groups.io``           | subscribe to mailing list               |
    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+
    | ``simon.c.blyth@gmail.com``                      | any questions                           |
    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+


.. comment

    | https://github.com/simoncblyth/opticks           | releases                                |
    +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------------------------------+

.. s5_talk::

    Klop

    of neutrino detectors.




.. comment

    Concurrent Opticks Server Ideas
    ---------------------------------


    .. class:: normal

        Problem: enormous load range

        * handful to billions of photons
        * fraction of a second to minutes

        python FastAPI asyncio.semaphore


        * requests divided into lanes (eg small/medium/large) of fixed concurrency eg 4/2/1
        * VRAM carved into fixed size photon buffers corresponding to concurrency config

        **Handling more photons than available slots**

        * slice gensteps, do multiple kernel launches
